title: Upgrade @anthropic-ai/sdk to expose output_config.effort and mid_conv_system
description: Bump @anthropic-ai/sdk from 0.39.0 to a version whose published types include the top-level output_config.effort field and the mid_conv_system content block, update package-lock, and fix any breaking changes in the Anthropic adapter so it compiles and keeps all existing behavior (including the prompt-caching helpers) intact. No new feature behavior is added here; this sprint is purely the dependency upgrade plus compile/regression fixes.

successCriteria.description: package.json declares an @anthropic-ai/sdk version (e.g. 0.100.x) whose installed types expose output_config.effort and the mid_conv_system content block, and package-lock.json is regenerated to match.
grep of node_modules/@anthropic-ai/sdk type declarations finds both an effort field under output_config and a mid_conv_system block type after the upgrade.
npm run typecheck exits zero against the upgraded SDK with no new TypeScript errors in the Anthropic adapter.
npm run lint and npm run build both exit zero after the upgrade.
Every existing provider unit test passes, including the prompt-caching tests in anthropic.test.ts, with only the documented flaky 37-tool-count baseline failures remaining.
@anthropic-ai/sdk remains imported only inside src/providers/anthropic.ts, verified by grepping the src tree for the package name.

nonGoals: Do not add the effort field or mid_conv_system handling in this sprint; sprints 3 and 4 own those.
Do not change adapter request/response behavior beyond what the upgrade requires to compile and keep tests green.
Do not introduce unjustified `any` casts to paper over SDK type changes.
Do not modify other provider adapters except where the upgrade forces a compile fix.
stopConditions: Stop when the upgraded SDK compiles, typecheck/lint/build exit zero, and the full suite is green except the flaky baseline.
Stop when the installed @anthropic-ai/sdk types include output_config.effort and the mid_conv_system block.
definitionOfDone: @anthropic-ai/sdk is upgraded in package.json and package-lock to a version whose installed type declarations expose output_config.effort and the mid_conv_system content block; the Anthropic adapter compiles with its existing behavior preserved (prompt-caching helpers included); npm run typecheck, lint, and build all exit zero; every provider unit test passes apart from the documented flaky baseline; and @anthropic-ai/sdk stays imported only inside the adapter.
assumptions: The current latest @anthropic-ai/sdk (0.100.1) types both new fields; if an earlier version already does, that is acceptable as long as both are present.
Sprint 1 may or may not be merged first; this sprint does not depend on it.
generatorNotes: Upgrade @anthropic-ai/sdk to the current latest (0.100.1) or the minimum version that types output_config.effort and the mid_conv_system block. Update package.json and run npm install to regenerate package-lock. This is a LARGE version jump (0.39 -> ~0.100): expect possible breaking changes in messages.create params, the content-block param/response unions, usage fields (input_tokens/output_tokens and the cache_read_input_tokens/cache_creation_input_tokens names), the stop_reason union, and the cache_control / TextBlockParam / CacheControlEphemeral types that the recently-added prompt-caching helpers (buildCachedSystem, attachMessageBreakpoints) rely on. Fix imports/usages in src/providers/anthropic.ts ONLY (keep the SDK out of every other file). Verify the prompt-caching unit tests still pass unchanged. Confirm the new types are present: `grep -rn 'mid_conv_system\|output_config\|effort' node_modules/@anthropic-ai/sdk/resources`. Tolerate ONLY the 2 flaky 37-tool-count baseline failures. If a clean compile needs more than mechanical fixes, prefer correct typed code over `any`; document anything non-obvious. Conventional commit: bober(sprint-2): upgrade @anthropic-ai/sdk for Opus 4.8 API fields.
evaluatorNotes: Read package.json — confirm @anthropic-ai/sdk version raised and package-lock updated. Run `grep -rn 'output_config\|effort\|mid_conv_system' node_modules/@anthropic-ai/sdk/resources` and confirm both effort (under output_config) and mid_conv_system types are present. Run npm run typecheck, lint, build (all exit 0). Run npm run test — anthropic.test.ts (7) and factory.test.ts (26) and all other provider suites must pass; only the 2 flaky 37-tool-count baseline failures are tolerated; confirm the caching tests still pass (behavior unchanged). Run `grep -rln '@anthropic-ai/sdk' src` and confirm the only match is src/providers/anthropic.ts. Check the adapter diff for any unjustified `any`.
